titleStmt.title: SII 29.80: original edition by P. N. Narasimha Murthy
No. 80. (A.R. No. B. 579 of 1961-62.) KURAḌI, MANVI TALUK, RAICHUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: Slab built into the wall inside the temple called Nelaguḍi.

This slab is broken and very much damaged. Hence the information obtained from this record is dis-jointed. Probably it recorded a land grant for the [worship and] offerings to the god [name lost] and an oil-mill perhaps for the burning of Perpetual lamp. Probably a wet land of the extent of kamma 200 measured in a particular rod of measurement [name lost] was also granted. Names of the donor and donee are lost. There is a mention of mahājanas.

In the date portion it mentions 47th year (probably Chalukya Vikrama year). In the cyclic year only letter to is clear. Probably the intended year was Śubhakritu. The 47th year of Chālukya Vikrama era holds good with the cyclic year Śubhakritu. The name of the month is lost but the tithi seems to be ba. 5. Though the exact date of the record cannot be made out, 1122-23 A.D. may be stated as its year. On this basis it can be said that the record belongs to the reign period of Chālukya Vikramāditya VI. [Refer No. 78 above].

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī
matu cā(ḷukya)
da nālvattē (ḷaneya)
tu saṁvatsara
ḷa paṁca
svasti śrī
Aśēṣa mahājana
naivēdyakkaṁ
gāṇavoṁdu
sakala
vīsa Alliṁ mūḍalu Ā kōlalu
gadde kamma Innū ṟu
Digital edition of SII 29.80 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
